title: Luca

jokes.text: Like most of my problems, it all started at Moe's...
jokes.setup: Homer needs to explain his predicament
jokes.punchline: The classic sitcom callback that most problems start at the bar
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-awareness about his poor decision-making, well-integrated with character

jokes.text: It'll be nice to let someone else decide when I drink-- too much pressure.
jokes.setup: Drinking game rules where you drink when the news says 'senator'
jokes.punchline: Barney finds relief in having external triggers for his alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific dark humor - only Barney would find decision-free drinking appealing

jokes.text: Not 'sauce,' 'source.' Source! S-A-U-R-C-E!
jokes.setup: Moe trying to be an anonymous news tipster
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces 'source' as 'sauce' and has to spell it out
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe malapropism, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: We have heard from a very reliable sauce news involving multiple senators
jokes.setup: Moe just corrected himself about saying 'sauce' instead of 'source'
jokes.punchline: The newscaster immediately makes the same mistake
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that escalates the wordplay confusion

jokes.text: Hmm? Oh, damn, the plaster's flaking again.
jokes.setup: Moe's bar is falling apart during the drinking game
jokes.punchline: Casual reaction to obvious structural decay
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag establishing the decrepit bar environment

jokes.text: I'm a kid again!
jokes.setup: Drunk Homer goes to playground swings
jokes.punchline: His childlike enthusiasm contrasts with his adult size and drunkenness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer regression humor, sets up the playground mishap

jokes.text: Oh, the only way out is in! Hey, look at me! I'm going up a slide!
jokes.setup: Homer is stuck in playground equipment
jokes.punchline: His illogical solution and excitement about going backwards on a slide
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's backwards logic making the situation worse

jokes.text: Don't worry, Homer, I'll call the cops! / I'll write my senator. / Senator! Senator!
jokes.setup: Homer needs help being rescued from playground equipment
jokes.punchline: Their 'help' triggers more drinking from the senator game
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows how their drinking game makes them useless in a crisis

jokes.text: Oh, am I gonna die on a playground, like some uncoordinated child?
jokes.setup: Homer is trapped in children's playground equipment
jokes.punchline: He's insulting exactly what he's become - an uncoordinated adult acting like a child
jokes.explanation: Good self-referential irony where Homer lacks awareness of his own situation

jokes.text: But I did learn something from all this: the sprinklers in this park come on at 3:00 a.m.
jokes.setup: Homer reflects on his traumatic playground experience
jokes.punchline: The only lesson he learned is trivial sprinkler timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - expects profound life lesson, gets mundane sprinkler schedule

jokes.text: Don't want to be seen with you when you're hitting bottom. / At least we hope it's bottom. / Don't worry, it's bottom, all right.
jokes.setup: Kids are embarrassed by Homer's playground incident
jokes.punchline: Dark escalation about whether this is rock bottom, with Homer's grim certainty
jokes.explanation: Good escalating family dysfunction with dark undertones about Homer's life

jokes.text: What about the one your ma used on you? / It, uh, broke on my buttocks.
jokes.setup: Willie suggests using Skinner's mother's switch for discipline
jokes.punchline: Reveals Skinner's mother disciplined him so hard it broke the switch
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Skinner's overbearing mother relationship

jokes.text: Serves you right for eating jam straight out of the jar.
jokes.setup: Skinner admits the switch broke on him
jokes.punchline: Willie's random moral judgment about jam consumption
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur that adds to Willie's bizarre moral code

jokes.text: Damn it! That's not even a swivel chair.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to do dramatic chair spin
jokes.punchline: His chair doesn't swivel, ruining his attempted dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy failure where Skinner's dramatic gesture is undermined by cheap furniture

jokes.text: Skate, skate as fast as you can. But I'll catch you-- I'm the Principal Man!
jokes.setup: Skinner chasing Bart on roller skates
jokes.punchline: Parody of Gingerbread Man rhyme with 'Principal Man'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay pun that fits Skinner's tendency toward theatrical declarations

jokes.text: That's the kind of education you're missing.
jokes.setup: Skinner just made a silly rhyme while chasing a student
jokes.punchline: He frames his nonsense as educational
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner character moment - his pompous justification for silly behavior

jokes.text: The one place my car can't go.
jokes.setup: Bart encounters a hill while fleeing in a car
jokes.punchline: Obvious statement about car limitations treated as revelation
jokes.explanation: Weak observational humor that states the obvious without much wit

jokes.text: Some cause happiness wherever they go; others whenever they go. / Uh, P.S. Your car is totaled.
jokes.setup: Lucas offers an elegant Oscar Wilde quote
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with crude practical information about the crashed car
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of highbrow literature with lowbrow practical concerns

jokes.text: Well, I can't have cops sniffing around here. Some of them might be girls.
jokes.setup: Snake explains why he can't have police around his hideout
jokes.punchline: His concern is about female officers, not getting arrested
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Snake's skewed priorities and casual sexism

jokes.text: I always thought that if I'd had braces, I'd have smiled more.
jokes.setup: Snake explains robbing for his son's braces
jokes.punchline: Touching parental reflection from a violent criminal
jokes.explanation: Good character depth - shows Snake's humanity beneath criminal exterior

jokes.text: Ay, caramba!
jokes.setup: Snake shows his supposedly great smile
jokes.punchline: His smile is apparently so bad it elicits Bart's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Uses Bart's catchphrase effectively to imply Snake's smile is horrifying

jokes.text: You can't buy me with a candy bar that has coconut.
jokes.setup: Police try to bribe Bart with candy for information
jokes.punchline: Bart has specific confectionery standards for his corruption
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bart's particular corruption standards

jokes.text: Eddie! Prepare my litter! Lou, you are responsible for my oxygen, food, water and cleaning! You have to clean me. I clean you. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Wiggum prepares for a mountain expedition
jokes.punchline: Escalating demands culminating in mutual cleaning arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Wiggum's incompetent leadership and weird priorities

jokes.text: Stolen bowling shoes, 3-D glasses, Krusty standee and this 'cage' for my pet. / What do you feed him? / My A.D.D. meds.
jokes.setup: Bart lists his 'criminal' possessions to bond with Snake
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's drugging his pet with ADHD medication
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prescription drug abuse that escalates Bart's 'criminal' credentials

jokes.text: I'm busy that night. / Yeah, lucky you.
jokes.setup: Snake offers Homer tickets to school musical
jokes.punchline: Snake's deadpan agreement that avoiding school plays is lucky
jokes.explanation: Decent parent humor about the pain of attending children's performances

jokes.text: What record? Stupidest death?
jokes.setup: Lucas claims he's going for a record while choking on hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Bart's assumption it's a Darwin Award category
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with efficient setup/punchline structure from Bart's perspective

jokes.text: That's French for 'hello.' / Actually, I think it...
jokes.setup: Lucas says 'Merci beaucoup'
jokes.punchline: Claims it means 'hello' when it obviously means 'thank you'
jokes.explanation: Shows Lucas's ignorance and social awkwardness through basic French mistake

jokes.text: A fat kid with a dream? I can't compete with that.
jokes.setup: Lucas introduces himself as competitive eater
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizes the unbeatable sympathy factor
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about societal sympathy for underdog narratives

jokes.text: All the glamour foods: pizza, boiled eggs, chicken wings and the big enchilada, which is not enchiladas but hot dogs.
jokes.setup: Listing competitive eating foods
jokes.punchline: The 'big enchilada' being hot dogs instead of enchiladas
jokes.explanation: Plays on 'big enchilada' idiom while introducing competitive eating terminology.

jokes.text: That's gonna hurt coming out.
jokes.setup: Lucas explains what Kobayashi says in Japanese
jokes.punchline: Translation reveals crude biological consequence
jokes.explanation: Straightforward scatological humor that fits competitive eating context

jokes.text: I won't be using the correct term then.
jokes.setup: Lucas explains 'gurgitator' is the correct term for competitive eater
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate rejection of the pompous terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - cuts through pretension with characteristic directness

jokes.text: He's just Ralph with a dream. / The dream of not ralphing.
jokes.setup: Bart compares Lucas to Ralph Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Pun on Ralph's name and 'ralphing' (vomiting)
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay connecting Ralph Wiggum to vomiting while commenting on Lucas's eating goals

jokes.text: But I'm sure I could totally change and fix him.
jokes.setup: Lisa realizes Lucas is problematic
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls into classic 'I can fix him' relationship trap
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Lisa's tendency toward problematic relationship patterns

jokes.text: Where's the gift receipt? Where's the packet of desiccant that says 'Do not eat'? And, believe me, you shouldn't.
jokes.setup: Lisa investigates Bart's suspicious new gaming system
jokes.punchline: Lists missing legitimate purchase signs, with personal desiccant warning
jokes.explanation: Good detective work humor with Lisa's characteristic thoroughness and personal anecdote

jokes.text: You can't spell 'crime' without 'me.' / C-R-I... / There it is, at the end!
jokes.setup: Bart makes wordplay about crime containing 'me'
jokes.punchline: Lisa starts to disprove it, but Bart points out 'me' is literally at the end
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection where the obvious objection actually proves the point

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'Luca-dollar.' That's my competition name.
jokes.setup: Lucas introduces himself to Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: His competitive eating name replaces 's' with 'dollar'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that establishes Lucas's attempts at competitive eating persona

jokes.text: Then that makes me the Jackie Robinson of the sport, and you are the racist Philadelphia manager. / Quit comparing me to Ben Chapman.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to join competitive eating despite heart problems
jokes.punchline: Escalates to specific civil rights analogy that Marge knows too well
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer's dramatic comparisons and Marge's weary familiarity

jokes.text: Never saw the pork eat the beans before.
jokes.setup: Homer observing competitive eating practice
jokes.punchline: Reversal of normal pork-and-beans relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever observational reversal about Lucas eating beans, comparing him to pork

jokes.text: Justin Blobber over there doesn't remind you of anyone? / Women marry their fathers, Marge. So you just might be meeting your future 'ton-in-law.'
jokes.setup: Patty implies Lucas reminds them of Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'ton-in-law' pun combining weight reference with family relationship
jokes.explanation: Good pun that works on multiple levels - weight, family relations, and self-awareness

jokes.text: Are you calling me not fat? / Maybe you just haven't found the right food, huh?
jokes.setup: Lucas suggests Homer isn't cut out for competitive eating
jokes.punchline: Homer takes offense at being called 'not fat' rather than bad at eating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - being insulted by not being called fat rather than incompetent

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Brain freeze! Oh, my God. Kick me in the head till I pass out. Harder! I'm still conscious!
jokes.setup: Homer tries competitive ice cream eating
jokes.punchline: Extreme brain freeze leads to desperate requests for head trauma
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's pain response with characteristic over-the-top solutions

jokes.text: Twenty. Thanks, babe. Sorry you had to drop out of college to feed me full-time.
jokes.setup: Lucas's girlfriend serves him pork chops
jokes.punchline: Reveals she sacrificed her education to enable his eating habits
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about toxic relationship dynamics in competitive eating world

jokes.text: Better be a 64 gig. / It's full of lame apps: Bully Avoider, Nosebook, Insta-Grandma.
jokes.setup: Bart expects high-end stolen phone
jokes.punchline: Phone has pathetic parody apps for uncool kids
jokes.explanation: Good social media parody apps that establish the phone's previous uncool owner

jokes.text: This was stolen from Milhouse! From my backpack. Where Puppy Goo-Goo sleeps.
jokes.setup: Milhouse reveals his phone was stolen
jokes.punchline: Escalating details about his childish backpack setup
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific detail showing Milhouse's childish possessions

jokes.text: I never would've suspected the one criminal in town.
jokes.setup: Learning that Snake is behind the thefts
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's sarcastic shock at the obvious suspect
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment showing rare moment of sarcastic competence

jokes.text: Careful. It's uncut syrup. They give it to horses before they race.
jokes.setup: Snake offers Homer a Squishee
jokes.punchline: Describes convenience store drink like illegal drugs
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating sugar like performance-enhancing drugs

jokes.text: Albert Knickerbocker Aloysius Snake
jokes.setup: News report about Snake's arrest
jokes.punchline: Snake's ridiculously formal full name contrasts with his criminal persona
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing the incongruity of Snake's proper background

jokes.text: We had a law stating 'Three strikes and you're out.' But I thought it was 'You're out.' So I let people go. So now it's 'Four balls and you walk. Right to the electric chair.'
jokes.setup: Quimby explains the new harsh sentencing law
jokes.punchline: Confused baseball metaphors leading to execution
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire using baseball metaphors for criminal justice confusion

jokes.text: I was crushed when I lost my dad and all he did was move to the Holiday Inn. I can still see him smoking on the balcony. He looked like he missed something. Maybe me.
jokes.setup: Bart explains why he doesn't want Snake to die
jokes.punchline: Touching backstory about his father's separation
jokes.explanation: Rare emotional depth from Bart showing vulnerability beneath his tough exterior

jokes.text: I didn't say there'd be a sleepover. / Too bad, 'cause I'm wearing my pajamas under my clothes. And my swimsuit under those. One day it'll all pay off.
jokes.setup: Milhouse mentions sleepover detectives
jokes.punchline: Reveals his over-preparedness layering strategy
jokes.explanation: Great Milhouse character moment showing his excessive preparation and optimism

jokes.text: Oh, I can fake attentive. I've been watching a football game this whole discussion.
jokes.setup: Marge says he can't fake being attentive
jokes.punchline: Homer proves her point by admitting he wasn't listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-defeating logic that proves Marge's point while missing it entirely

jokes.text: She can smash a salad.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to take Lisa to a crab-smashing restaurant
jokes.punchline: His solution for her vegetarianism is violence against vegetables
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic that completely misses the point of vegetarianism

jokes.text: She might marry someone like me? You think that would be bad.
jokes.setup: Marge implies Lisa shouldn't marry someone like Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's wounded realization of what Marge is really saying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's occasional self-awareness and hurt feelings

jokes.text: Marge, you are comparing me to the most infuriating thing in the newspaper!
jokes.setup: Marge compares Homer to the Daily Jumble puzzle
jokes.punchline: Homer's outraged reaction to being compared to a word puzzle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's wounded pride at puzzle comparison

jokes.text: I'm sleeping on Flanders' couch tonight. Ours is crap.
jokes.setup: Homer storms out after fight with Marge
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's going to Flanders because of furniture quality, not principle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misdirection - choosing spite location based on comfort rather than anger

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Oh, my God! Oh, my God! / Hello? / Uh, hello. Lisa? I know your brother and... Oh! Stupid, stupid, stupid!
jokes.setup: Homer's friends encourage him to call Lisa for a date
jokes.punchline: He panics and tries to pretend he's not her father
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's nervousness about asking his daughter on a date

jokes.text: I got a date with my daughter! / Yeah, we all been there. / No need to act like you just invented air conditioning.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about daughter date
jokes.punchline: Bar friends are unimpressed, treat it as routine
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's excitement by unimpressed bar crowd, air conditioning line is absurd

jokes.text: I got a date with my daughter! / Yeah, we all been there. No need to act like you just invented air conditioning.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrates getting a date with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Moe's deadpan deflation of Homer's excitement
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan from Moe with creative metaphor for common experience

jokes.text: The instructions are in Swedish, Chief. We got this from IKILLYA. / Yo, I totally speak Swedish. / Fluently? / Um, ja.
jokes.setup: Police struggle with Swedish electric chair instructions from 'IKILLYA'
jokes.punchline: Snake claims fluency but only knows 'yes' in Swedish
jokes.explanation: Good IKEA parody with Snake's transparent lie about language skills

jokes.text: But Chief, we got guns; he's got a little wrench. / It's cold!
jokes.setup: Lou questioning releasing armed criminal
jokes.punchline: Wiggum immediately surrenders to tiny tool
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum cowardice, surrendering to minor discomfort despite advantage

jokes.text: If I get caught, it's suicide by cop. / Oh, I don't like the sound of that. / It means I get you to shoot me, dude. / Yeah, still sounds like a lot of paperwork.
jokes.setup: Snake explains his suicide by cop plan
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's concern is about administrative burden, not death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Wiggum response prioritizing bureaucracy over human life

jokes.text: You look like an NBA coach.
jokes.setup: Homer gets dressed up for his date with Lisa
jokes.punchline: His formal attire makes him look like a basketball coach
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Homer's awkward formal wear

jokes.text: You look like an NBA coach.
jokes.setup: Homer dressed up for dinner
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific comparison to basketball coach
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific comparison that implies Homer looks professional but not elegant

jokes.text: What is the normal amount of entrees per person? / Eh, one, sir. / I mean for adults. / One. / No, I mean for adult males. / Yes. I repeat, eh, one.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering dinner with Lisa
jokes.punchline: His confusion about normal portions escalates through demographics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's inability to understand normal human portion sizes

jokes.text: Can the red sauce be cow blood?
jokes.setup: Homer orders vegetarian lasagna to accommodate Lisa
jokes.punchline: Tries to make it more appealing to himself with blood substitution
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic trying to carnivore-ize vegetarian food

jokes.text: Homie, please, control your jealousy. This is your daughter.
jokes.setup: Marge interrupting Homer's date with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer treats it like Marge is jealous of another woman
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable humor from Homer's misreading of the situation.

jokes.text: Remember that sewing machine you say I never use? Well, I sold it and bought this dress.
jokes.setup: Homer admires Marge's dress
jokes.punchline: She vindicated her 'useless' sewing machine by selling it
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline about marriage arguments and vindication

jokes.text: Thanks, Frenchy. / I'm not French, I'm just pissy.
jokes.setup: Homer calls the waiter 'Frenchy'
jokes.punchline: Waiter clarifies his attitude, not nationality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment from service worker with economic punchline

jokes.text: Thanks, Frenchy. / I'm not French, I'm just pissy.
jokes.setup: Homer thanks waiter with French nickname
jokes.punchline: Waiter's clarification about his attitude vs. nationality
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of stereotypical rude French waiter - he's just American and cranky

jokes.text: My new goal is to become whatever Adele is. Just call me Pound-Uca-Dollar.
jokes.setup: Lucas abandons competitive eating
jokes.punchline: New career goal with weight-based pun on his name
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay continuing Lucas's weight-based identity transformation

jokes.text: You know how to whistle? Like with your mouth?
jokes.setup: Lisa offers to teach Lucas to whistle
jokes.punchline: His amazement at the concept reveals how sheltered he is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucas's innocence and lack of basic skills

tmdb_overview: Lisa dates a competitive eater; Bart benefits from helping convict Snake Jailbird escape from a jam.
